prompts.title: Where does Berlin pull you today?
prompts.situation: The city looks easy from a hotel lobby. Then the U-Bahn heat, the Spree breeze and one sudden cloud make the decision for you.
prompts.guideNote: My practical move: choose one area and leave yourself a way out if the weather turns.

options.label: Stay outside
options.advice: Use parks, canals and open squares before the light gets too flat.

options.label: Museum shelter
options.advice: Pick one museum cluster, not three scattered stops.

options.label: Kiosk pause
options.advice: A cold drink and ten quiet minutes can save the next two hours.

options.label: Find water shade
options.advice: The Spree, Landwehrkanal or a shady bridge will usually beat a random square.

prompts.title: What does a first Berlin day need most?
prompts.situation: Most first days fail from one small thing: too many stops before the city has started to make sense.
prompts.guideNote: I would anchor the day around Alexanderplatz, Museum Island and Hackescher Markt, then stop pretending you can see everything.

options.label: A clear route
options.advice: Start with a route you can explain in one sentence.

options.label: Food first
options.advice: Do not make your biggest decisions while hungry.

options.label: One story
options.advice: One good story makes the next five streets easier to read.

options.label: A quiet break
options.advice: A planned pause is better than an accidental collapse.

prompts.title: The sky looks unsure. What wins?
prompts.situation: Berlin rain rarely asks politely. It arrives just when you are between stations and not quite hungry enough to stop.
prompts.guideNote: My move is simple: stay near useful transit and choose a Plan B before you need it.

options.label: Risk the walk
options.advice: Fine if your next shelter is under ten minutes away.

options.label: Cafe delay
options.advice: Best when the rain looks sharp but short.

options.label: Museum pivot
options.advice: Good when the whole afternoon looks wet.

options.label: Ride through it
options.advice: A tram window can be a better Berlin view than a soaked pavement.

prompts.title: Which Berlin neighborhood mood is strongest?
prompts.situation: A good Berlin day is rarely the whole city. It is usually one district doing exactly what you needed.
prompts.guideNote: I would not chase six neighborhoods. Pick the mood, then stay with it long enough to notice details.

options.label: Mitte clarity
options.advice: Use it when you want the city to explain itself quickly.

options.label: Kreuzberg drift
options.advice: Better when you have time to wander instead of ticking boxes.

options.label: Neukoelln evening
options.advice: Best when the day wants food, streets and a slower finish.

options.label: Prenzlauer calm
options.advice: Useful when you need Berlin without fighting Berlin.

prompts.title: Your Berlin transport instinct today?
prompts.situation: The map looks logical until one platform sign, one lift closure or one tired decision changes the whole trip.
prompts.guideNote: My rule: if the route needs three clever transfers, make it simpler.

options.label: S-Bahn long hop
options.advice: Good when distance matters more than door-to-door perfection.

options.label: U-Bahn tunnel
options.advice: Best when you can avoid a transfer and arrive near the stop.

options.label: Tram window
options.advice: Take it when the view matters and you are not late.

options.label: Just walk
options.advice: Often right inside Mitte, especially when two stations are too much effort.

prompts.title: What should you follow first?
prompts.situation: Berlin gives you food, history and strange little streets at the same time. The mistake is trying to obey all of them.
prompts.guideNote: I would choose the anchor first, then let the rest orbit around it.

options.label: Classic snack
options.advice: Use it as a quick reset, not the whole food plan.

options.label: Doener stop
options.advice: Better when you need calories before another long walk.

options.label: A serious stop
options.advice: Do it when you can give the place full attention.

options.label: Market browse
options.advice: Works best when the weather and timing are already on your side.

prompts.title: What kind of Berlin evening is this?
prompts.situation: Berlin nights can be huge, but the best evening is often the one you can still enjoy tomorrow morning.
prompts.guideNote: My honest advice: choose the night you actually have energy for.

options.label: One beer outside
options.advice: The right bench can beat an overplanned bar crawl.

options.label: Late door gamble
options.advice: Only worth it if you accept the queue and the no.

options.label: River walk
options.advice: Good when the day was full and you still want Berlin.

options.label: Protect tomorrow
options.advice: Not glamorous, but often the smartest travel decision.

prompts.title: What should you collect today?
prompts.situation: It is easy to photograph Berlin and still miss what was happening right in front of you.
prompts.guideNote: I would take the photo, then put the phone down for the next street.

options.label: One strong photo
options.advice: Make one image you actually want to remember.

options.label: One good story
options.advice: A story will outlast a dozen rushed pictures.

options.label: Tiny detail
options.advice: Berlin often explains itself through small traces.

options.label: A route idea
options.advice: The best souvenir can be knowing where to go next.
